hooks.command: sid=$(cat /dev/stdin | jq -r '.session_id // empty' 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$sid" ] && exit 0; f="/tmp/claude-reads-$sid"; count=0; [ -f "$f" ] && count=$(cat "$f"); echo $((count + 1)) > "$f"

sid=$(cat /dev/stdin | jq -r '.session_id // empty' 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$sid" ] && exit 0; f="/tmp/claude-reads-$sid"; count=0; [ -f "$f" ] && count=$(cat "$f"); rm -f "$f"; [ "$count" -ge 8 ] && echo '{"decision": "block", "reason": "You read '"$count"' files this session. If you explored unfamiliar code without documenting findings, update ~/doc/projects/<name>/index.org now (Org-mode markup, no :ID: properties). If docs are already updated or reads were routine, continue."}' || echo '{}'

echo 'Context was compacted. Reminder: Check ~/doc/projects/ for existing knowledge before re-exploring code. Read ~/doc/CLAUDE.md for knowledge base structure. This is a Guix dotfiles repo — see CLAUDE.md for structure.'
